title: polygala vulgaris var. serpyllifolia
intro: Polygala vulgaris var. serpyllifolia, also known as common polygala or wild dodder, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Polygalaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris var. serpyllifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
